trade.summary: Corea del Sud is Australia's fourth-largest trading partner. Australia exports LNG, coal, iron ore, and copper; Corea del Sud exports vehicles, electronics, ships, and petrochemicals. The Corea del Sud-Australia Free Trade Agreement (KAFTA), in force since 2014, has deepened trade. Corea del Sud is Australia's third-largest LNG customer.

diplomatic.summary: Australia-Corea del Sud relations are elevated to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership. Both are democracies, G20 members, and Stati Uniti treaty allies. Alignment on Cina policy, North Corea del Sudn denuclearisation, and Indo-Pacific security is strong. Annual leaders summits reflect the depth of the relationship. Both nations cooperate extensively in the UN and multilateral forums.

military.summary: Both countries are Stati Uniti treaty allies with deepening bilateral military cooperation. Australian and Corea del Sudn forces operate together through UN Command in Corea del Sud. Joint exercises include Exercise Haedoli-Wallaby (land forces) and naval cooperation. Growing defence industry cooperation, including potential collaboration on future combat vehicles.

financial.summary: Substantial Corea del Sudn investment in Australia across energy, real estate, and technology sectors. Australian investment in Corea del Sud is growing. Long-term LNG and coal supply contracts provide a stable financial foundation. Corea del Sudn conglomerates (chaebols) have significant Australian assets.

energy.summary: Corea del Sud is Australia's third-largest LNG customer and major coal importer. Australia supplies approximately 25% of Corea del Sud's LNG. Both countries are developing green hydrogen cooperation with Corea del Sud targeting Australian hydrogen imports under its national hydrogen strategy.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific collaboration, particularly in materials science, renewable energy, and biotechnology. Australian universities enrol significant Corea del Sudn student populations. Joint research initiatives supported by government science agreements. Cooperation in hydrogen technology and battery materials is expanding rapidly.
